title: Dog Legs Crossing Over While Walking: Causes and When to See a Vet
meta_desc: If your dog's legs are crossing over (scissoring gait), it may indicate a neurological or orthopedic issue. Learn about urgency and next steps.
content_html:
TL;DR: A dog crossing its legs while walking, known as a scissoring gait, typically indicates a coordination issue called ataxia and should be evaluated by a veterinarian within 24 to 48 hours.

Why is my dog crossing its legs while walking?

When a dog's legs cross over one another while walking, often referred to as a 'scissoring gait,' it typically indicates a lack of coordination known as ataxia. This occurs when the brain, spinal cord, or inner ear cannot properly communicate with the limbs, or when significant weakness in the hips or spine causes the legs to drift inward toward the midline.

Is my dog's scissoring gait a medical emergency?

  • The urgency level for this symptom is Medium, meaning your dog should be evaluated by a veterinarian within 24 to 48 hours.
  • This is not usually an immediate life-threatening emergency unless it is accompanied by the inability to stand, sudden paralysis, or signs of extreme pain.
  • If the condition is progressing rapidly, seek veterinary care sooner.

How does a video of my dog's gait help with veterinary triage?

  • Capture a clear video of your dog walking, as these symptoms can often be intermittent.
  • A video allows the vet to observe the specific pattern of the 'cross-over' to identify which limbs are most affected.
  • Visual documentation helps the veterinary team determine if the issue is neurological or musculoskeletal before the physical exam begins.

Clinical Context (Merck Veterinary Manual)

Crossing of the hind legs while walking in dogs can be attributed to various causes, including fibrotic myopathy of the semitendinosus muscle or restrictions caused by annular ligaments, adhesions, or severe fibrosis. In fibrotic myopathy, a mechanical lameness results in the affected limb being pulled back and down quickly before the end of the protraction phase, creating the impression of the foot slapping down on the ground, most obvious at a walk. It is critical to correctly determine the cause of the lameness, because treatment varies greatly depending on the cause.

Chapter: Neurology, Infectious Diseases

Source: The Merck Veterinary Manual, 11th Edition (Page 1049)

faqs.question: What are common causes of legs crossing in dogs?
faqs.answer: Common causes include intervertebral disc disease (IVDD), hip dysplasia, degenerative myelopathy, or vestibular disease.

faqs.question: Should I limit my dog's activity?
faqs.answer: Yes. Until you see a veterinarian, it is best to limit your dog to short, leashed bathroom breaks and avoid jumping, stairs, or rough play to prevent further injury.

faqs.question: Can this be treated?
faqs.answer: Many conditions causing a scissoring gait are treatable with medication, physical therapy, or in some cases, surgery, depending on the underlying cause diagnosed by your vet.
